strategy: 100,000 health, 50% resist to everything, 101% damage boost — full team fight: hitter, two supports, healer.

Do nothing aggressive early: after every spell he casts, he hits himself for 5,000 until he reaches 60,000 health. Spend the opening rounds shielding, blading the hitter, and Feinting — the first 40,000 damage is free.
Survive the every-2nd-round Superhero Strike casts — healer pre-heals into even rounds; 225 critical and 16% pierce make these hurt.
His round-1 Chi aura guts his outgoing healing (-300%), so your damage sticks — no Doom and Gloom needed.
Once he sits at 60,000, stop stalling: his Berserk/Frenzy stacking from Mandar snowballs, and long fights get lethal.
Bring pierce for the 50% universal resist (no boost school exists) and burst the remaining 60,000 with a fully bladed hit through Feints.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he gives himself a 'Chi!!' aura at -300% outgoing healing — he cannot meaningfully heal himself for the whole fight.
After every spell he casts, he hits himself for 5,000 until he's at 60,000 health — the first 40% of the fight is free. Stall, shield, and let him do the work.
His self-hits don't consume blades, traps, or shields — your Feints stay on him for the real hit.
Every 2nd round a Superhero Strike spell comes out — rotate shields and keep health above the burst line on even rounds.
If he already has Frenzy and rolls Mandar, he upgrades to Frenzy Quicken — his output snowballs the longer the fight runs.
He otherwise stacks Berserk, then Frenzy — once his self-damage stops at 60,000, every extra round makes him hit harder. Don't stall past that point.
